title: Hachure TrustReport — Waivers extension
$comment: OPTIONAL extension companion to the Waivers profile (waivers.md). A producer that adopts the waivers profile may attach a derived waiver-validity projection to its TrustReport: a per-claim verdict answering 'is this assumed claim's accepted-gap waiver valid'. This schema is the strict validation surface for such a report — it references the neutral core field set (trust-report.schema.json#/$defs/core) and adds exactly the two waiver-validity fields, closing the combined set with unevaluatedProperties:false. A consumer that expects waiver-validity fields validates against THIS schema; a consumer that does not validates against trust-report.schema.json (which rejects these fields as unknown). Core trust-report semantics and the status function are unchanged: waiver validity is a downstream consumer projection, never a status-derivation input. The verdict vocabulary mirrors the consumer-derivation outcomes described in waivers.md.

waiverValidityFunctionVersion.$comment: Version of the waiver-validity derivation algorithm, mirroring statusFunctionVersion's role for the status function. Lets a consumer know which derivation produced the verdicts.

waiverValidityByClaimId.$comment: Map of claim id -> derived WaiverValidity. Keys are arbitrary claim ids (including ids such as "__proto__"); consumers MUST treat this as a plain data map of own keys.

approverAuthenticated.$comment: Whether the waiver's approver identity is cryptographically authenticated. Under the waivers profile as defined today this is always false (approved_by is free text — the disclosed unauthenticated-approver residual in waivers.md); typed as boolean rather than const:false so a future identity-binding profile (e.g. Assurance L1/L2) can set it true without a breaking schema change.

waiver.$comment: The waiver facts the verdict was derived from, when present. Loosely typed here (the normative field shape lives in the waivers profile, on claim.metadata.waiver).

incompleteFields.$comment: For an incomplete-waiver verdict: which of the three required waiver fields were missing or malformed.
